mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects broad economic strength and robust corporate hiring, leading to higher aggregate demand, improved earnings expectations, and positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment is a core component of NBER business cycle dating.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between 3-month changes in PAYEMS and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates past a key threshold, it forces the central bank to adopt a more hawkish stance. This increases the discount rate for future earnings and dampens investor sentiment, causing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the median 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a very low unemployment rate (UNRATE) and decelerating retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a late-cycle economy where labor costs are high but consumer demand is faltering. This squeezes corporate profit margins, leading to negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is below 4.5% AND 3-month change in RSAFS is negative.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when the trigger is active fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of a severe slowdown in domestic demand. This negative signal is amplified when monetary policy is restrictive (high FEDFUNDS), as high borrowing costs exacerbate the housing downturn and its spillover effects.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a highly interest-rate sensitive sector.
failure_condition: The negative return profile following a HOUST contraction trigger is not statistically more severe when FEDFUNDS is above 4% compared to when it is below 2% (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A positive surprise in industrial production (INDPRO) growth signals robust manufacturing and business investment, leading to immediate upward revisions of near-term corporate earnings and positive short-term equity market reactions.
candidate_trigger: Month-over-month INDPRO growth exceeds consensus forecast by 0.3pp or more.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An inverted yield curve, proxied by the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) being significantly higher than long-term growth expectations (proxied by a constant), signals restrictive monetary policy that historically precedes recessions and bear markets.
failure_condition: Periods where FEDFUNDS is above 5.0% do not have lower 60-day forward returns on average than periods where FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarter-over-quarter reading in real GDP (GDPC1) confirms a broad-based contraction in economic activity. This confirmation of weakness triggers investor de-risking and negative returns as recession probabilities are revised upwards.
evidence_refs: fact:Two consecutive quarters of negative GDP growth is a common rule-of-thumb for recession.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive median returns in the 20 trading days following the official release of a negative GDPC1 figure.

mechanism_narrative: A swift rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) from a cyclical low (the 'Sahm Rule' intuition) is a highly reliable, real-time indicator of a recession's onset, causing a rapid repricing of equities to the downside.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of UNRATE is > 0.5pp above its 12-month low.
failure_condition: Triggered instances are not followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of cases over the last four business cycles.
